description: In C, prefer established idioms (manual memory ownership conventions, errno checks, and goto-cleanup patterns) over generic rewrites.
body: In C, the ecosystem has settled idioms — manual memory ownership conventions, errno checks, and goto-cleanup patterns — and changes that ignore them add risk and review friction. Use when adding or modifying C code near existing patterns. Do not impose a foreign style or rewrite working code into a generic shape, and avoid abandoning nearby conventions. Actions: read the surrounding code, follow its manual memory ownership conventions, errno checks, and goto-cleanup patterns, keep the change minimal, then run clang-tidy, cppcheck, and -fanalyzer warnings and the unit binary plus valgrind on the changed translation unit to confirm fit. Validation signals include a small diff consistent with neighbors, a clean lint pass, and passing tests. Risk boundary: a broad idiom migration is its own reviewed task; escalate rather than bundling it into an unrelated change.
